metadata.name: Time Spent in Nature
metadata.description: Regular exposure to natural environments (greenspace) is associated with reduced cardiovascular and all-cause mortality, with optimal benefits at 120-300 minutes per week.

sources.citation: Twohig-Bennett C, Jones A. The health benefits of the great outdoors: A systematic review and meta-analysis of greenspace exposure and health outcomes. Environmental Research. 2018;166:628-637.
White MP, et al. Spending at least 120 minutes a week in nature is associated with good health and wellbeing. Scientific Reports. 2019;9:7730.

metadata.mechanismOfAction: Reduced inflammation, stress reduction through cortisol regulation, increased physical activity, improved immune function, air quality benefits, psychological restoration

applicability.notes: Meta-analysis of 143 studies. Benefits observed across urban parks, nature trails, and wilderness settings.

points.citation: 1 hour per week shows modest benefit
2 hours per week (threshold for significant benefit)
Additional time shows minimal added benefit
